S.text: It got clear fast, then this week it hasn't changed much.
Still a little hazy, looking through a smudged window.
Continue the ketorolac once daily; it's helping guard against swelling at the back of the eye that could also blur things.
Should I be worried the vision plateaued?
If it worsens or you get pain, call us. Thank you, Doctor.

O.text: He says the vision improved and then seemed to plateau.
Uncorrected distance vision is 20/40 in the right eye, and 20/25 in the left.
The cornea is mostly clear with just a trace of residual edema inferiorly — that will finish clearing.
Anterior chamber shows 1+ cell and trace flare, so a little inflammation is lingering, which can soften the vision.
Tono-Pen: IOP 19 mmHg OD, 17 mmHg OS.

Pressure is within normal range, 19 on the right. Given the lingering inflammation, rather than taper this week I want to keep the prednisolone acetate 1% at four times daily in the right eye for one more week to settle that anterior chamber down. The exam explains it — trace edema plus a bit of inflammation. We'll see you at post-op month one for your refraction, and I expect a much crisper right eye by then.

A.text: POD#8 s/p cataract extraction OD; VA plateau attributed to lingering anterior chamber inflammation plus trace residual corneal edema; posterior capsule clear; well-positioned IOL. (Note laterality: right eye is OD.)

P.text: You can stop the moxifloxacin now — a week of antibiotic is enough.
You can stop the night shield now at week one.
